title: P0: Wire 'lob run <circuit>' full pipeline + report
description: Compose the stages (SKiDL run -> parse -> validate -> simulate -> verify -> BOM) into 'lob run', print a per-stage pass/fail report, and exit non-zero on any failure. This is THE working loop. DESIGN.md 2.2.
acceptance_criteria: 'lob run examples/rc_lowpass' runs all stages end-to-end, prints a report, exits 0 on success / non-zero on failure
close_reason: Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

title: P0: Textbook verification — assert simulated cutoff vs 1/(2piRC)
description: Compare the simulated -3dB point to the analytic cutoff and fail the stage if outside tolerance. This is what makes Phase 0 a *proof* of the loop, not just a run. DESIGN.md 14.1, 1.2.
acceptance_criteria: passes for correct RC values; deliberately-wrong values fail with a clear diff; tolerance configurable
close_reason: core::verify::check_rc_cutoff computes analytic 1/(2piRC) from parsed R/C values and asserts the simulated -3dB cutoff within a configurable tolerance. End-to-end: expected 1000.97 Hz vs simulated 1000.96 Hz (0.001% err). Failure path unit-tested (wrong cutoff → failed outcome w/ diff). Non-single-RC → skipped.

title: P0: SPICE deck generation + ngspice runner (AC analysis)
description: Generate a SPICE netlist from the Circuit model, run ngspice (AC sweep for the RC filter), and parse the frequency response. DESIGN.md 5.1.
acceptance_criteria: ngspice runs headless on generated deck; AC magnitude vs frequency parsed into data lob can assert on
close_reason: core::spice generates a SPICE deck from the Circuit (pin→net inversion, GND→0, R/C/L support, IN/OUT/GND harness), runs 'ngspice -b', and parses the AC response (freq, vdb). AcResult exposes passband gain + interpolated -3dB cutoff. Verified: 601-point sweep on the RC filter. 9 spice/units tests.

title: P0: KiCad netlist parser -> Circuit (CircuitSource impl)
description: Parse the KiCad netlist emitted by SKiDL into the core Circuit model as the first CircuitSource implementation. Captures parts (refdes, value, footprint) and nets (connections/pins). DESIGN.md 3.3.
acceptance_criteria: parses the RC netlist into parts+nets; round-trip unit test on a fixture netlist; cargo test passes
close_reason: core::netlist parses the KiCad S-expr netlist into the Circuit model (first CircuitSource producer): parts (refdes/value/footprint/libsource) + nets (name + pins), deterministically sorted. Self-contained S-expr reader, no new deps. Wired as stage 2 of 'lob run' (parses the real generated netlist: 2 parts, 3 nets). 14 unit tests incl. round-trip on an RC netlist fixture; fmt+clippy clean.

title: P0: SKiDL runner stage (script -> netlist + ERC)
description: Rust stage that shells out to run a SKiDL script in a subprocess, captures the generated KiCad netlist file and the ERC output. Handles non-zero exit / missing interpreter gracefully. First real pipeline stage.
acceptance_criteria: given examples/rc_lowpass, produces a netlist artifact + captured ERC text; errors surfaced, not panicked
close_reason: SKiDL runner (core::skidl::SkidlRunner) shells out via the venv python, runs in a scratch work_dir so artifacts stay out of the repo, captures netlist + ERC report, surfaces missing-interpreter/non-zero-exit as StageError (not panic). Wired as stage 1 of 'lob run'; verified end-to-end on examples/rc_lowpass.py. 10 unit tests, fmt+clippy clean.

title: P0: RC low-pass filter demo circuit in SKiDL
description: Write the first demo circuit (single-pole RC low-pass) as a standalone SKiDL script under examples/, with documented component values and the textbook -3dB cutoff fc = 1/(2*pi*R*C). This is the known-good fixture the whole loop is validated against. DESIGN.md 3.1, 14.1.
acceptance_criteria: SKiDL script runs and emits a KiCad netlist; expected fc recorded alongside; ERC clean
notes: RC low-pass at examples/rc_lowpass.py: R1=1k, C1=159n -> fc=1/(2piRC)~=1.001kHz. Values are the single source of truth; verify stage (P0.7) recomputes fc from parsed R/C rather than a hard-coded number. SKiDL emits a clean KiCad S-expr netlist (0 ERC errors). The 2 ERC WARNINGS are benign single-pin-net notices on the open IN/GND ports of a 2-port filter block; the sim stage (P0.6) wraps it in an AC source/ground testbench.
close_reason: Toolchain pinned + documented (docs/TOOLING.md, requirements.txt), tool discovery in core::tools, 'lob doctor' preflight implemented and verified; RC low-pass SKiDL circuit emits a clean netlist end-to-end.

title: P0: Tooling decision — SKiDL + ngspice + KiCad availability
description: Confirm and document the external toolchain the local loop shells out to: Python+SKiDL (netlist + ERC), ngspice (sim), KiCad/pcbnew (layout, stretch). Pin versions, document install, and decide how lob discovers them (PATH vs config). Blocks the runner work.
design: Pinned toolchain (verified on macOS/darwin 24): ngspice 45.2 on PATH (required); SKiDL 2.2.3 in repo .venv, pinned in requirements.txt, defaults to kicad9 backend so needs KICAD9_SYMBOL_DIR (required); KiCad symbol libs from KiCad 10 app bundle at /Applications/KiCad/KiCad.app/Contents/SharedSupport/symbols (compatible .kicad_sym); kicad-cli 10.0.0 at app-bundle MacOS/ path, NOT on PATH, optional at Phase 0. Discovery: legion-of-bom-core::tools searches PATH then macOS fallbacks; 'lob doctor' renders the preflight. Full detail in docs/TOOLING.md.
acceptance_criteria: versions pinned + documented; lob has a way to locate each tool; a 'lob doctor'-style check or documented preflight exists
close_reason: Toolchain pinned + documented (docs/TOOLING.md, requirements.txt), tool discovery in core::tools, 'lob doctor' preflight implemented and verified; RC low-pass SKiDL circuit emits a clean netlist end-to-end.

title: lob CLI skeleton (clap) with run subcommand stub
description: Wire the lob binary with clap: global flags (circuit path, verbosity/logging), and a 'run <circuit>' subcommand that currently just resolves the path and prints planned stages. This is the orchestration entrypoint the Phase 0 stages plug into.
acceptance_criteria: lob run <path> parses args and exits 0; lob --help lists run; logging initialized
close_reason: Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).

title: Scaffold Rust cargo workspace + Rust .gitignore
description: Create a cargo workspace: crates/core (legion-of-bom-core library) and crates/cli (lob binary). Add Rust-appropriate .gitignore (target/, etc.) early, on top of the existing beads/dolt ignores. Goal: 'cargo check' passes on an empty-but-real skeleton.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check passes; workspace has crates/core + crates/cli; .gitignore ignores target/; lob --help runs
close_reason: Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).

title: Core domain model + CircuitSource trait boundary
description: Define the internal circuit model (Circuit, Part, Net, Pin, RefDes, value/footprint) and a thin CircuitSource trait that pipeline stages consume, per DESIGN.md 2.3/3.3. Only implementation for now is 'parsed SKiDL netlist', but downstream stages must depend on the trait, not SKiDL types, so the eventual IR extraction is one new impl not a rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: CircuitSource trait defined returning parts+nets; concrete Circuit type; unit tests construct a small circuit; cargo test passes
close_reason: Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).

title: Epic: Foundation & Scaffold
description: Rust cargo workspace (legion-of-bom-core lib + lob CLI), CircuitSource trait boundary, pipeline stage traits, error handling, and the dev loop (cargo check > test > build, fmt, clippy). Everything downstream depends on this. See DESIGN.md sections 2.2-2.4, 3.3.
close_reason: All foundation tasks complete; scaffold builds and the trait boundaries are in place.

title: Epic: Phase 0 — End-to-end demo loop (local-only)
description: Prove the full pipeline on textbook circuits (RC low-pass filter, then non-inverting op-amp gain stage) with known-good answers before any real product design. SKiDL -> netlist -> parse -> validate (ERC) -> ngspice sim checked vs textbook values -> BOM. Entirely local, no network. DESIGN.md 14.1, 1.2.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: the full 6-stage loop (SKiDL -> parse -> validate -> simulate -> verify -> BOM) is proven end-to-end on two textbook circuits with known-good answers — RC low-pass (cutoff vs 1/2piRC, 0.001%) and non-inverting op-amp (gain vs 1+Rf/Rg, 0.05% via the component's real subckt model). Local-only, self-verifying, non-zero exit on failure. Stretch task P0.11 (layout scaffolding) superseded by the Layout epic's kicad-ipc-rs task (j54.5).

title: Layout 6.9: PanelSpec trait — format-agnostic seam (Eurorack impl only)
description: Same move as the deferred circuit IR (3.3), applied to panels: define a thin PanelSpec trait NOW, implement it fully for EURORACK ONLY (what's shipping), and defer pedal/rack/500-series impls until one is actually being built. Trait shape: dimensions in mm (each format's native units — HP/U, pedal enclosure size class, rack U × 19in-fraction, 500-series fixed slot — translate to mm INSIDE the impl, never exposed as universal concepts to the rest of the pipeline), a mounting-hole list, and a list of anchored cutouts (position, rotation, footprint reference). Section 7 DXF export and Section 6's layout loop both read panels THROUGH this trait, not assuming Eurorack. Rationale for not unifying speculatively: the four formats aren't similar enough, and 500-series is mechanically the odd one out (audio I/O via a card-edge connector into the shared lunchbox bus, not front-panel jacks) — forcing it into the anchored-jack-pot cutout model would be WRONG, not just incomplete. DESIGN.md 6.9; cf. 6.1, 7.1.
acceptance_criteria: a PanelSpec trait (mm dimensions + mounting holes + anchored cutouts) with a complete Eurorack impl; the layout loop + DXF export consume panels only through the trait; no Eurorack-specific concepts (HP/U) leak into pipeline code; other formats unimplemented on purpose

title: Parts: SPICE model sourcing + verification (part_spice_models)
description: SPICE models are a DISTINCT parts-library artifact from pinout/ratings (DESIGN.md 3.5): sourced differently (from the MANUFACTURER directly, e.g. TI's product page — not a distributor API field) and verified differently. Multi-channel packages need a WRAPPER subcircuit (raw mfr model is single-channel; a dual op-amp instantiates it twice with matching pin order — cf. kicad_builtin_opamp_dual/quad). Verification is NOT 'read the pin number' — it's 'run a known textbook case and confirm the simulated result matches', the same evidentiary bar Phase 0 held its RC/op-amp demos to. Stored in a part_spice_models table. Builds on the Phase 0 component-model resolver seam (okm.8).
acceptance_criteria: part_spice_models table; a manufacturer SPICE model can be imported + wrapped for multi-channel; a model is marked verified only after a textbook-case sim matches within tolerance; the generic SPICE generator consumes these the same way it consumes symbol-resolved models

title: BOM: priced BOM CSV output (qty × pricing + totals)
description: Emit the priced BOM CSV: refdes, qty, MPN/value, footprint, unit price, extended price, and a BOM total. This is the concrete 'Mouser-priced BOM/CSV' deliverable. DESIGN.md 9.1.
acceptance_criteria: lob emits a priced BOM CSV for a circuit whose parts have MPNs; columns + total correct; opens cleanly in a spreadsheet
close_reason: Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

title: BOM: Mouser Search API integration (live price/stock by MPN)
description: Query Mouser's Search API for live unit price (at quantity breaks) and stock per part, keyed by MPN from the global parts library. Single-vendor to start — the multi-vendor JLC-basic/extended/LCSC fallback is deliberately deferred. DESIGN.md 9.x.
acceptance_criteria: given a part MPN, returns live Mouser price + stock; handles not-found / API errors gracefully; testable with explicit MPNs before parts-library MPNs land
close_reason: Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

title: BOM: priced BOM model + cost roll-up (extend the Circuit BOM)
description: Extend the existing Circuit-derived BOM (Phase 0 delivered grouping by value/footprint, qty, refdes) with unit price, per-line extended cost, and a BOM total. Layout-independent — derived from the netlist. DESIGN.md 9.1.
acceptance_criteria: BOM lines carry unit price + extended cost; a total is computed; still derived purely from the parsed circuit
close_reason: Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

title: Epic: BOM & sourcing — Mouser-priced BOM/CSV
description: The layout-INDEPENDENT half of BOM/PCBA, split out of Phase 4 on purpose. A BOM (parts, quantities, pricing) is derivable straight from the verified circuit definition (SKiDL netlist + global parts library) with ZERO dependency on layout — unlike CPL, which needs XY coordinates from a placed board. Delivers the near-term high-value output: a Mouser-priced BOM CSV. Deliberately EXCLUDES: CPL (layout-dependent, lives with Layout), JLCPCB BOM/CPL file formatting (manual zip/CSV upload path already works, no urgency), and multi-vendor fallback (nice-to-have once single-vendor lands). DESIGN.md 9.1, 9.4.

title: Parts: component-carried SPICE macro-models (generic instantiation, no per-device branches)
description: A part carries its SPICE model (primitive R/C/L, or subckt + pin->terminal map + params + .include library) resolved from the component, not hardcoded in the generator. Source now: KiCad symbol Sim.* fields (Sim.Device/Name/Library/Pins) — which SKiDL's netlist path drops, so a symbol-model resolver is needed; source later: the Dolt parts library. SPICE generator becomes model-agnostic: instantiate whatever each part declares (+ supply rails for models that need vcc/vee). Unblocks P0.10 op-amp sim. Prevents the 'if is_ideal_opamp' anti-pattern in the generator. DESIGN.md 3.5, 5.1; MCP.md 1.
acceptance_criteria: generator has no per-device conditionals; op-amp sim uses the component's real subckt (or a model resolved FROM the component); supply rails handled; RC + op-amp both simulate through the same generic path
close_reason: Implemented: components carry their SPICE model, resolved FROM the component (core::symbols reads the KiCad symbol's Sim.Device/Name/Library/Pins — which SKiDL's netlist drops — and the .sp subckt terminal order). core::spice::SpiceModel + generic generate_ac_deck instantiates subckt (X-line + .include) or primitive (R/C/L) with supply rails, ZERO per-device conditionals. Verified: RC via primitive path, op-amp via the real kicad_builtin_opamp subckt, both through the same generic path. S-expr reader promoted to shared core::sexpr.

title: Parts: define_circuit resolves parts by MPN from the global library
description: Wire the circuit-definition path (hand-written SKiDL, lob CLI, or MCP agent) to resolve parts by MPN against the global library rather than model memory or per-project files. First use of a new MPN triggers one-time human verification; later projects read verified data for free. DESIGN.md 3.5, MCP.md 1.2.
close_reason: MPN resolution shipped. model::Part carries mpn (extracted from the netlist's MPN field); PartsLibrary::resolve_circuit returns per-part ResolutionStatus (NoMpn/Unknown/Unverified/Verified) against the library; 'lob parts resolve <circuit>'. Verified end-to-end: a SKiDL circuit with MPN=LM13700 resolves ⚠ unverified → ✓ verified. Unblocks zya.2 (Mouser pricing by resolved MPN) and okm.4 (verification gate). 41 core tests.

title: Parts: verified_by_human gate in legion-of-bom-core (blocks layout + generate_bom)
description: THE gate. Before layout (Sec 6) or generate_bom (Sec 9) run — via CLI or MCP — core checks: every referenced part has a parts row with verified_by_human=TRUE, AND the circuit's open-questions checklist has no unresolved items. Fail -> return the specific blocking items, don't run. Lives in core (MCP.md 1.4/2.2), not the MCP wrapper; MCP tool and 'lob layout' hit the same check. DESIGN.md 4.3.
close_reason: Verification gate shipped: PartResolution::blocks_verified_use + 'lob parts gate <circuit>' exits non-zero if any MPN-bearing part isn't human-verified — the enforceable structural block that layout/real-BOM-ordering call. Verified fail(exit1)->pass.

title: Parts: fetch_datasheet — cited pin/rating extraction into the library
description: Real fetch-and-parse (never a 'model already knows this part' shortcut): pull structured pin/footprint/rating data from a CAD library per trust order, or extract from PDF (pin tables, absolute-max ratings, package) when no library entry exists. Every part_pins/part_ratings row carries cited_page back to source. verified_by_human starts FALSE. MCP.md 1.2-1.3.
notes: First source landed: KiCad official library (core::fetch::fetch_from_kicad, 'lob parts fetch <mpn> --source kicad') — pins + datasheet URL into the parts library. FINDING: the KiCad LM13700 symbol leaves bias/output/buffer pins unnamed, so a single CAD library isn't authoritative — validates the multi-source trust order + human verification. REMAINING: distributor-API source (JLCPCB, creds in .env) for authoritative pins/manufacturer + datasheet URL; PDF-page citations (cited_page); ratings extraction; then the cross-check (okm.5).
close_reason: fetch_datasheet delivered across two structured sources: KiCad official library (core::fetch::fetch_from_kicad — pins + datasheet URL) + JLCPCB distributor API (core::jlcpcb HMAC-signed, verified live — datasheet + structured ratings + MPN by LCSC code). Records keyed by MPN, unverified until confirmed; fetches MERGE across sources (CLI merge_fetched) so pins+ratings compose. PDF-page-citation extraction tier tracked as legion-of-bom-okm.11.

title: Parts: distributor CAD-library sourcing (trust order) + PDF fallback
description: Sourcing order (MCP.md 1.1): distributor-official CAD library (DigiKey in-house KiCad lib, JLCPCB/EasyEDA/LCSC via existing Parts API) -> broader CAD library (SnapEDA/Ultra Librarian, check verification badge) -> PDF datasheet extraction as fallback. PDF URL only ever from a distributor API record, never general web search. Structured library pin-name<->pin-number mapping is a better source than parsing PDF prose.
notes: Tooling directive (LIBRARIES.md §4): DRIVE existing import tools rather than write per-source scrapers — KiCad PCM (many libs ship a repository.json for direct install), JLC2KICAD_lib (EasyEDA/LCSC → KiCad), Import-LIB-KiCad-Plugin (SnapEDA/SamacSys/Ultra Librarian), SamacSys Library Loader. Adopt the KICAD_3RD_PARTY env convention (one subfolder per source) to keep sources separated by trust/license. Full curated source list + license status in LIBRARIES.md; license policy tracked separately.
close_reason: Distributor/CAD sourcing + trust order implemented: KiCad official CAD library + JLCPCB distributor API (per the DESIGN 3.5 trust order). Import-tooling directive (drive KiCad PCM / JLC2KICAD_lib per LIBRARIES.md) documented in notes. PDF fallback split to legion-of-bom-okm.11.

title: Parts: Dolt-backed global parts library schema (parts/part_pins/part_ratings + citations)
description: Schema per MCP.md 1.2: parts(mpn key, manufacturer, datasheet_url from distributor API, fetched_at, verified_by_human, verified_at/by); part_pins(pin_number, pin_name, cited_page); part_ratings(rating_name, value, unit, cited_page). Dolt-backed, cross-project, versioned via refs like inventory (DESIGN.md 2.6). Distinct from inventory tables (Phase 4).
close_reason: Dolt-backed parts library schema + read/write interface shipped. core::parts: parts/part_pins/part_ratings (mpn as natural key, citations via cited_page); PartsLibrary shells out to the dolt CLI (init + schema + transactional upsert + JSON reads + versioning commit), matching the project's shell-out pattern. PartRecord/PinRecord/RatingRecord, verified_by_human gate field, default cross-project dir (LOB_PARTS_DIR override). 'lob parts list/show/add/verify' CLI. Verified end-to-end (LM13700) + unit + real-dolt round-trip test. Unblocks okm.3 (fetch_datasheet), okm.7 (MPN resolution), okm.9 (SPICE models).

title: Epic: Global parts library & human-verification gate (core)
description: Structural fix for the LM13700-pinout-from-memory class of mistake. A global, Dolt-backed parts library (parts/part_pins/part_ratings, every fact carrying a citation) that circuit definition resolves parts against by MPN — never model/training memory, never a per-project file. verified_by_human gates real use: layout and generate_bom REFUSE to run on any part not human-confirmed at least once. Enforced inside legion-of-bom-core (identical for CLI and MCP), not bolted onto the MCP wrapper. DESIGN.md 3.5, 4.3, 9.4; MCP.md 1-3.
notes: PAUSED (2026-07-22): the remaining tasks (okm.9 SPICE model sourcing, okm.5 cross-check, okm.11 PDF fallback) are refinements that only bite once REAL boards with REAL parts flow through the pipeline — which needs the downstream stages (layout → board → gerbers/panels) built first. Deferred; resume when Phase 1 (slew limiter, first real board) or the layout epic actually exercises verified parts. The library is functional now (schema, KiCad+JLCPCB fetch, resolve, gate, verify, Mouser pricing).

title: Layout 6.5: Iterative layout loop engine (place -> route -> check -> repair)
description: Guided iterative repair over FREE (non-anchored) components/nets only — not general autorouting. 1) auto-place free parts seeded near anchored neighbors (decoupling cap near its IC); 2) auto-route non-critical nets against the mode's cost weights; 3) check violations: electrical DRC, mechanical/3D collision (6.7), critical-net rules (tagged nets short+direct?); 4) targeted repair (not full re-place) + recheck; 5) exit is NOT 'zero violations' — nets the loop can't confidently resolve are surfaced for manual routing (6.8), never silently solved. Each attempt is a natural git commit (repos are git-native) giving free, diffable history. DESIGN.md 6.5.
acceptance_criteria: loop runs on a demo board: places free parts, routes non-critical nets, checks + repairs, and terminates by resolving what it can and surfacing the rest

title: Layout 6.6: KiCad IPC integration via kicad-ipc-rs (board file + routing readback)
description: The mechanics of driving KiCad pcbnew from legion-of-bom-core to turn the netlist/IR into a .kicad_pcb with footprints placed. Hardened successor to the Phase 0 layout-scaffolding stretch task. DESIGN.md 6.6.
design: DECISION (DESIGN.md 6.6): use kicad-ipc-rs against KiCad's IPC API, NOT the SWIG Python pcbnew bindings (deprecated in KiCad 9, removed in KiCad 11 — SWIG would force a rewrite on upgrade). IPC is KiCad's forward-looking interface (where official kicad-python lives). v1 connection mode: ATTACH to a running KiCad GUI instance (IPC in KiCad 9/10 has no headless mode yet; avoid Docker/xvfb, esp. on macOS) — fine given local-first single-user. Upgrade path (not a maybe): 'kicad-cli api-server' ships headless IPC in KiCad 11; switch is which socket kicad-ipc-rs connects to, not a rewrite. Mechanics: SKiDL netlist -> kicad-ipc-rs creates/updates board, places anchored (6.1) + free (6.5) footprints, drives routing, reads violations back over IPC (6.5 step 3); Gerbers via 'kicad-cli pcb export gerbers' (already headless, separate from the GUI-attach requirement).
acceptance_criteria: legion-of-bom-core produces a .kicad_pcb from a parsed circuit with footprints present; deterministic + reopenable in KiCad

title: Layout 6.4: Critical-net tagging in SKiDL, carried through to IR
description: Manual (confirmed decision, auto-detection rejected): the author tags nets needing analog-careful treatment at definition time (feedback net, high-Z input, matched stereo pair). Shape: a critical() wrapper / tag arg on the net in SKiDL, carried through the netlist/IR so the layout loop reads it without re-deriving. Touches the circuit-definition and parse layers. DESIGN.md 6.4.
acceptance_criteria: a net can be tagged critical in SKiDL; the tag survives netlist->Circuit parse and is queryable on the model

title: Layout 6.2: 2-layer ground-pour convention
description: Hard v1 constraint: 2 layers. Convention: bottom = solid ground pour, top = signal+power, via stitching to ground as needed. Replaces most manual star-ground work with a low-impedance return 'for free'; critical-net tagging (6.4) is the escape hatch where the pour isn't enough. DESIGN.md 6.2.
acceptance_criteria: board generation applies bottom ground pour + top signal/power + via stitching by default

title: Layout 6.1: Format profiles (PanelProfile) with anchored connectors
description: Four v1 form factors — Eurorack, Guitar Pedal, Rack Mount, Desktop — each a PanelProfile: mounting-hole pattern, panel dimension convention (Euro HP / pedal size class / rack U / desktop footprint), and a library of standard connectors with fixed footprints + 3D models. Connectors (jacks, pots, switches) are PLACEMENT-ANCHORED from the panel spec, not placed by the optimizer — this shrinks free placement to just support components and is what makes the loop tractable vs NP-hard. DESIGN.md 6.1.
acceptance_criteria: a PanelProfile type with the 4 profiles; anchored connector positions derived from panel spec and exposed to the layout loop
notes: Refined by 6.9 (legion-of-bom-j54.10): don't design all four format profiles now. 6.1 becomes the EURORACK implementation of the PanelSpec trait (mounting-hole pattern, anchored jack/pot/LED connector library). Other formats' PanelSpec impls are deferred until actually built; 500-series in particular must NOT be forced into the anchored-front-panel-jack model (card-edge bus). Depends on the trait (legion-of-bom-j54.10).

title: Epic: Layout & DFM — iterative layout loop
description: Detailed expansion of DESIGN.md section 6 (the layout part of Phase 2 MVP hardening). Not general autorouting: constrain the problem to 4 format profiles with placement-ANCHORED connectors, a 2-layer ground-pour convention, and an analog/digital cost function, so a guided place->route->check->repair loop over only the FREE components/nets becomes tractable. Unresolvable critical nets are surfaced for manual routing, never silently heuristic'd. Depends on the Phase 0 circuit model + netlist parser + KiCad scaffolding.

title: P4: JLCPCB API integration (quote / order / track)
description: DESIGN.md 9.3.

title: P4: BOM accuracy/verification hardening (airtight)
description: BOM correctness has to be airtight before ordering. DESIGN.md 9.4.

title: P2: PedalKernel simulation integration
description: Reuse PedalKernel's existing accuracy/harness infrastructure as a sim backend alongside ngspice. DESIGN.md 5.2.

title: P2: KiCad layout hardening (footprint mapping + placement)
description: Robust netlist->board: footprint assignment, deterministic placement scaffolding, round-trip. DESIGN.md 6.1.

title: P2: Expose pipeline over MCP server (crates/mcp)
description: Second interface after the CLI: an MCP server wrapping the SAME core lib so agents/tools can drive the pipeline. CLI/MCP-first per project direction; UI deferred. DESIGN.md 2.2.

title: P1: Run slew limiter through the Phase 0 loop; capture gaps
description: Push the real board through validate/sim/BOM. Log every place the textbook-circuit loop breaks on a real design; file follow-ups. DESIGN.md 14.2.

title: P1: Slew limiter circuit in SKiDL (OTA slew stage)
description: First real Puget Audio board as an OTA-based slew limiter, defined in SKiDL. DESIGN.md 14.2.

title: P0: Non-inverting op-amp gain stage demo (2nd circuit)
description: Second textbook circuit: non-inverting op-amp gain stage. Exercises the same loop with a different topology and a different analytic check (gain = 1 + Rf/Rg). Two circuits is the point where reuse patterns can start to emerge (DESIGN.md 3.4, 14.1).
acceptance_criteria: 'lob run' passes on the op-amp circuit; gain verified vs 1+Rf/Rg
close_reason: Non-inverting op-amp gain stage (examples/opamp_noninv.py, Rf=9k/Rg=1k -> gain 10) passes the full 6-stage loop: sim uses the component's real op-amp subckt (resolved from the symbol, not hardcoded), verify checks 1+Rf/Rg topologically (expected 10.000x / 20dB, simulated 9.995x -> 0.05% err, the real op-amp's finite-gain deviation). Second demo circuit exercises the loop on a different topology + active device. Built on okm.8.

title: P0: BOM generation from Circuit
description: Generate a BOM from the Circuit model: group by value/footprint, count quantities, list refdes. Human-readable table + CSV. First slice of the BOM pipeline (DESIGN.md 9.1).
acceptance_criteria: BOM for RC filter lists R, C with correct qty/refdes; CSV output validated in a test
close_reason: Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

title: P0: Validation stage — surface ERC results
description: Validation stage that consumes ERC output (and/or re-derives basic checks) and reports structured warnings/errors through the PipelineReport. First pass per DESIGN.md 4.1.
acceptance_criteria: ERC warnings/errors appear in the pipeline report; clean circuit reports pass
close_reason: Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

title: Pipeline stage traits + unified error type
description: Define composable stage traits (e.g. ValidateStage, SimulateStage, BomStage) operating over CircuitSource, plus a unified error/report type so 'lob run' can compose stages and surface pass/fail per stage. DESIGN.md 2.2.
acceptance_criteria: stage traits compile; a PipelineReport/Error type exists; a no-op stage can be composed in a test
close_reason: Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).

title: Epic: Phase 2 — MVP hardening (KiCad / PedalKernel / SPICE outputs)
description: Harden the must-have outputs: KiCad layout scaffolding, PedalKernel sim integration, SPICE. DESIGN.md 14.3, sections 5-6.

title: Epic: Phase 1 — Slew limiter (first real Puget Audio board)
description: First real product design through the pipeline (OTA slew stage), still local/localhost. DESIGN.md 14.2.
notes: Reference designs (LIBRARIES.md §3): benjiaomodular's ecosystem includes a shipped MiniVCA built around the LM13700 — the same OTA as the slew limiter — worth studying as a real reference design (topology + real part footprints/3D). Also protorack-kicad / nathanaelnoir / tristan-smith libs for Eurorack jacks/pots/LM13700 footprints + 3D models.

title: Parts: PDF datasheet fallback extraction (cited_page)
description: The trust-order fallback tier of fetch_datasheet: when structured CAD/distributor sources are incomplete (e.g. the KiCad LM13700 symbol leaves bias/output/buffer pins UNNAMED), extract pin tables + absolute-max ratings from the datasheet PDF and populate cited_page with the real page number. Sourced only from a distributor-API datasheet URL (which okm.3 now captures). Completes the pin-name gaps the CAD sources leave. DESIGN.md 3.5, MCP.md 1.
acceptance_criteria: given a part with a datasheet URL, extract pin names/numbers + abs-max ratings with cited_page; fills the gaps the KiCad symbol left for LM13700

title: P3: Panel order tracking (panel_orders Dolt table; lob panel status/mark-ordered)
description: DESIGN.md 7.5: neither SendCutSend nor OSH Cut exposes a public order API (upload-to-web-app only, unlike JLCPCB/Mouser/DigiKey) — so panel ORDERING stays manual (human uploads the generated DXF + places the order). What's automatable is TRACKING that manual step so it isn't lost. panel_orders table in the same Dolt store as inventory (module, dxf_path/commit ref, vendor, status: not_ordered|ordered|shipped|received, ordered_at, tracking_ref, notes). CLI: 'lob panel status <module>' reads state; 'lob panel mark-ordered <module> --vendor oshcut --tracking <ref>' updates it. Deferred-not-designed-away: if a vendor adds an API, mark-ordered becomes an API call updating the same table — no schema change.
acceptance_criteria: panel_orders table in Dolt; lob panel status + mark-ordered work; status transitions tracked; DXF path/commit recorded per order

title: Parts: license handling policy (metadata, not a pipeline gate)
description: Per LIBRARIES.md: a library's license is INFORMATIONAL metadata surfaced to the user, NOT a pipeline gate — legion-of-bom doesn't police how end users use a library in their own designs. The one real exposure is if legion-of-bom itself bundles/redistributes library files in its own repo (CC-BY-SA 'collection' clause → same-license + attribution retained). Open item: before we VOUCH for any 'license not confirmed' source (KiCad official is confirmed CC-BY-SA 4.0; DigiKey/JLCPCB/SnapEDA/Eurorack repos are unconfirmed) in docs or tooling defaults, do a real license check. See LIBRARIES.md.
acceptance_criteria: a decided policy: license as surfaced metadata not a gate; redistribution handled if we bundle; a checklist for confirming a source before it becomes a recommended default
close_reason: Policy decided (LIBRARIES.md): a library's license is informational metadata surfaced to the user, NOT a pipeline gate — legion-of-bom doesn't police end-user usage. Only exposure is redistribution (CC-BY-SA 'collection' clause if we bundle library files). Verify a source before vouching (KiCad official confirmed CC-BY-SA 4.0). No license enforcement in the pipeline.

title: BOM: accuracy/verification — airtight before ordering
description: BOM correctness must be airtight before any real order: right part, right qty, right footprint, cross-checked against the verified parts library. Supersedes the old Phase 4 'BOM accuracy hardening' task now that BOM is its own epic. DESIGN.md 9.4.
acceptance_criteria: BOM verified against the parts library; qty/footprint/MPN mismatches flagged; blocks nothing at Phase 0 but gates real ordering

title: MCP: submit_for_approval (human touchpoint); NO place_order tool
description: submit_for_approval packages design+BOM+cost+sourcing into a human-reviewable summary. CRITICAL: place_order is NOT agent-callable — no such MCP tool exists at all; ordering is a manual human action outside the MCP surface. Building one later is a deliberate separate decision, never a default the surface grows into. MCP.md 2.1.

title: MCP: gated tools — layout, generate_bom (inherit core verification gate)
description: layout (iterative loop, DESIGN.md 6) and generate_bom (live pricing/stock from JLCPCB/Mouser/DigiKey) are gated: require open-questions checklist clear + all used parts verified_by_human=TRUE. Gate is core-enforced (same as CLI), tool just inherits it and returns blocking items on failure. MCP.md 2, 2.2.

title: MCP: ungated tools — create_project, research_topology, search_part, fetch_datasheet, define_circuit
description: create_project (scaffold repo from PanelProfile), research_topology (real reference circuits + citations), search_part (distributor records incl datasheet URL), fetch_datasheet (into global parts library), define_circuit (SKiDL vs verified data). MCP.md 2.

title: MCP: pipeline tools — validate, simulate (reuse Phase 0 stages)
description: Expose the existing core validate + simulate stages as MCP tools. These already exist from Phase 0; this is the MCP wrapping. MCP.md 2.

title: MCP: server scaffold (crates/mcp) over legion-of-bom-core
description: Stand up the local MCP server crate wrapping the core library; nothing MCP-only, everything delegates to core (DESIGN.md 2.2). MCP.md 2.

title: Epic: MCP agent interface (crates/mcp)
description: Agent-facing MCP server over the SAME legion-of-bom-core (local-first, not hosted SaaS). Lets an agent go prompt -> research -> define -> simulate -> layout -> BOM autonomously, but NEVER place a real order (no place_order tool exists — human-only, outside the MCP surface). Nothing may look verified without being verified: gated tools inherit the core parts-verification gate. Full spec in MCP.md. Supersedes the thin Phase 2 'expose over MCP' stub.

title: Parts: human verification workflow (confirm extraction once per part)
description: Lightweight review that sets verified_by_human: present extracted pinout/ratings table + citation/link, human confirms or corrects once per PART (not per circuit — global reuse). Same shape as the slew-limiter open-questions checklist, tool-enforced instead of doc-enforced. MCP.md 3.
close_reason: Human verification workflow: 'lob parts show <mpn>' presents extracted pins/ratings/datasheet + status; 'lob parts verify <mpn> --by' confirms ONCE per part (global, reused across projects). Tool-enforced version of the slew-limiter open-questions checklist.

title: Parts: cross-check safety/money data (pins, abs-max) from 2 independent sources
description: For pin assignments and absolute-max ratings specifically (error -> physical damage / bad respin), fetch from two independent sources where possible (distributor CAD lib + manufacturer PDF) and FLAG mismatches rather than silently picking one. Proportional: 'nice to know' parametrics don't need this. MCP.md 1.5.

title: Layout: decide 3D model library sourcing for connectors + collision checks
description: Both anchored connectors (6.1) and mechanical collision checks (6.7) require 3D models of every component. Decide the source/format (KiCad packaged 3D libs, vendor STEP models, manufacturer libraries) and how they're associated with footprints in the pipeline. Blocks collision checking. DESIGN.md 6.1, 6.7.
acceptance_criteria: a decided source + format for 3D models and how they attach to parts/footprints

title: Layout 6.8: Manual layout escape hatch (surface unresolved critical nets)
description: Any net the iterative loop can't resolve with confidence (per the 6.5 exit condition) is flagged for manual routing rather than forced through a heuristic. Treated as a feature: RIAA/phono-style circuits are exactly where layout mistakes are audible and hard to diagnose, so surfacing 'this needs human judgment' clearly beats a silent bad auto-route. DESIGN.md 6.8.
acceptance_criteria: unresolved/low-confidence nets are reported as explicit manual-routing items, distinct from hard failures

title: Layout 6.7: DFM checks (JLCDFM electrical + mechanical/3D collision)
description: Two check kinds: standard electrical/manufacturing DFM (JLCDFM or equivalent: trace/space, hole sizes) AND mechanical collision checks using 3D models of every component — catch a jack body hitting a neighbor or a panel cutout misaligned with its PCB footprint before it's a physical mistake. DESIGN.md 6.7.
acceptance_criteria: board runs electrical DFM + 3D collision checks; violations reported through the pipeline report

title: Layout 6.3: Analog/Digital/Mixed mode + cost-function weighting
description: Project-level mode (manual, not auto-detected) selects violation-scoring weights for the loop. Analog: ground-pour integrity, critical-net proximity (feedback nets, decoupling caps near IC power pins), channel symmetry (stereo matching). Digital: trace length, via count, later controlled impedance / diff pairs. Mixed: both, with per-net critical tags (6.4) disambiguating. DESIGN.md 6.3.
acceptance_criteria: a mode setting selects a weight set; the loop's check step scores violations by the active weights

title: P4: Inventory data model in Dolt
description: Parts/boards/revisions/quantities on hand; Dolt as source of truth. DESIGN.md 11.1, 11.3, 2.6.

title: P4: Thru-hole distributor sourcing + kitting (LCSC/Mouser/DigiKey)
description: DESIGN.md 10.1-10.2.

title: P4: Multi-vendor sourcing rules (JLC basic/extended, LCSC fallback)
description: DESIGN.md 9.5.

title: P4: CPL (component placement list) generation
description: DESIGN.md 9.2.
notes: CPL is layout-DEPENDENT: it needs real XY placement coordinates from a board, so it depends on the Layout epic (j54) producing a placed board. It is NOT a BOM concern — the priced BOM (epic legion-of-bom-zya) is layout-independent and ships first.

title: P3: Mechanical fit checks vs PCB (jack/pot alignment)
description: DESIGN.md 7.4.

title: P3: Panel SVG output
description: DESIGN.md 7.2.
notes: Deprioritized (DESIGN.md 7.2): SVG is LOWER priority than DXF — DXF covers both target fab vendors (SendCutSend, OSH Cut). Defer SVG until a concrete need appears.

title: P3: Panel DXF output
description: DESIGN.md 7.1.
notes: Decision (DESIGN.md 7.1): panel outline + cutouts (jack/pot/LED/mounting holes) exported to DXF via kicad-cli's plot/export — the already-headless CLI path used for Gerbers in 6.6, NOT gated by the 'KiCad GUI running' IPC requirement. Geometry driven by the PanelProfile (6.1) + per-module physical layout (jack/pot/LED positions, HP width). DXF is the PRIORITY format: it's the upload input SendCutSend and OSH Cut actually want. Relevant community tooling: danroblewis/kicad-eurorack-tools ships a pcbnew plugin drawing eurorack-spec'd panel cutouts (LIBRARIES.md §3).

title: P2: Manufacturing outputs — Gerbers via KiCad + output validation
description: Generate Gerbers via KiCad and validate outputs before any submission. DESIGN.md 8.1, 8.3.

title: P1: Extend sim/validation for active + OTA parts
description: Whatever the RC/op-amp loop lacks for a real OTA slew stage (device models, multi-analysis). Scope emerges from the gap capture.

title: P0 (stretch): KiCad layout scaffolding (netlist -> .kicad_pcb)
description: Shell out to KiCad/pcbnew to turn the netlist into a board file with parts placed (not routed). Proves the layout stage exists end-to-end; manual layout still human-driven. DESIGN.md 6.1, 1.3.
acceptance_criteria: a .kicad_pcb is produced from the RC netlist with footprints present; opens in KiCad

title: Dev-loop hygiene: fmt/clippy config + documented workflow
description: Add rustfmt.toml and clippy lint config; document the dev loop (cargo check > cargo test > cargo build; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy) in README/CLAUDE.md so every change runs the same gates.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check clean; cargo clippy clean on scaffold; workflow documented
close_reason: Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).

title: Epic: Phase 4 — PCBA ordering + inventory (Dolt) + reorder automation
description: PCBA ordering (JLCPCB API quote/order/track), CPL (layout-dependent), multi-vendor sourcing fallback, thru-hole sourcing/kitting, inventory in Dolt, shared-component tracking, reorder automation, QC/revision. The layout-INDEPENDENT priced BOM was split into its own epic (legion-of-bom-zya); CPL stays here but now depends on the Layout epic. DESIGN.md 9-13.

title: Epic: Phase 3 — Panel outputs (DXF / SVG / KiCad)
description: Panel design outputs and mechanical fit checks against PCB (jack/pot alignment). DESIGN.md 14.4, section 7.

title: MCP: decide research_topology sourcing + novel-circuit fallback
description: Open questions (MCP.md): does research_topology search general web (with citation requirements) or a curated trusted set (mfr app notes, established design refs)? Unbounded web risks bad reference designs with the same false-confidence problem as datasheet recall. And: when no good reference exists for a novel circuit, does the agent refuse or proceed with lower confidence + more scrutiny at verification?

title: P5: Multi-tenancy + hosted remote-repo connections
description: Forestry.io-for-KiCad model against someone else's remote repo. DESIGN.md 2.4, 2.5.

title: P5: Decide auth model (WebAuthn passkeys vs GitHub OAuth vs other)
description: DESIGN.md 2.5 — deferred until local loop works.

title: P5: axum web backend wrapping core lib
description: Same core lib, HTTP surface. DESIGN.md 2.2, 2.5.

title: P4: QC/test procedure + board revision scheme + traceability
description: Bench/functional test before ship; silkscreen rev <-> git tag <-> IR version; which rev shipped where. DESIGN.md 13.

title: P4: Reorder automation (thresholds, lead-time, n8n, human-in-loop)
description: DESIGN.md 12.1-12.3.

title: P4: Shared-component tracking across SKUs
description: 2OPFM, PHRSR/SCANNER/EG/TVCA, future Winterbloom line. DESIGN.md 11.2.

title: P3: KiCad-native panel workflow
description: DESIGN.md 7.3.

title: Epic: Phase 5 — SaaS-ification (multi-tenancy, auth, hosted repos)
description: Multi-tenancy, auth (WebAuthn/OAuth TBD), hosted remote-repo connections, web dashboard (axum). Only after the local loop works end-to-end. DESIGN.md 14.6, sections 2.5.

title: P6: Direct-to-gerber output path
description: DESIGN.md 8.2.

title: P6: Native Rust circuit DSL targeting the IR
description: DESIGN.md 3.2.

title: P6: Extract DSL-agnostic circuit IR
description: Extract the canonical IR once a 2nd DSL is real; retrofit SKiDL onto it. DESIGN.md 2.3, 3.3.

title: P5: Web dashboard UI (defer; Slint or React/Svelte + shadcn)
description: UI deferred per project direction; pick stack when we get here (Slint likely). DESIGN.md 14.6.

title: Epic: Phase 6 — Direct-to-gerber + additional DSLs (stretch)
description: Direct-to-gerber output path and additional circuit DSLs incl. native Rust DSL targeting the extracted IR. Stretch. DESIGN.md 14.7, sections 3.2, 8.2.
